_description: 课程题目配置 - 从8节新家主线课程生成

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Nice! You know where your neighbors live! / 很棒！你知道邻居们住哪儿了！
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Next door' means 隔壁, 'right above you' means 你正楼上, 'downstairs' means 楼下。

questions.instruction: "Your heater's off." — What does Jake mean?
questions.correct_feedback: Correct! The heater is off — no heat! / 对了！暖气关着——没有暖气！
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Heater's off' means the heating is not working. / 'Heater's off' 意思是暖气没开。

questions.instruction: Jake: "The guy before you must've closed it when he left." 你接下来该问什么？
questions.correct_feedback: Good question! You want to know about the last person here. / 好问题！你想知道之前住这里的人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Jake mentioned 'the guy before you' — you'd naturally ask 'Who lived here before?' / Jake提到了'之前的人'——你自然会问'之前谁住这里？'

questions.instruction: "My son used to live with me." — Why did Mrs. Henderson's son move into 4B before you?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! Her son got a place of his own. / 对！她的儿子想要独立空间。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! She said 'my son' — her son lived in 4B for his own place. / 她说了'my son'——她的儿子住在4B因为想要独立空间。

options.label: Her son wanted his own place.
Her son didn't love Mrs. Henderson.
Mrs. Henderson's brother wanted to move into 4A.

questions.instruction: Mrs. Henderson is asking if you need blankets. Answer quickly!
questions.correct_feedback: Quick and polite! 'I think so' is a great soft answer. / 又快又礼貌！'I think so' 是很好的委婉回答。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! She asked about blankets — 'I think so, thank you' fits best. / 她问的是毯子够不够——'I think so, thank you' 最合适。

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! Now you can tell someone about the heating. / 完美！现在你可以告诉别人暖气的情况了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! The correct sentence is 'Your heater is off.' / 正确句子是 'Your heater is off.'（你的暖气关着。）

questions.instruction: 请跟读：Who lived here before?
questions.correct_answer: Who lived here before.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Great pronunciation! You sound natural. / 发音很棒！听起来很自然。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: WHO lived HERE before? Stress the bold words. / 再试一次：重读WHO和HERE。

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Great! You understand how Jake visits you. / 很好！你理解了Jake怎么来找你的。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Knocks on your door' = 敲你的门, 'comes round' = 来访, 'downstairs' = 在楼下。

questions.instruction: Jake: "Let's go." You walk out together. Jake says: "Taco truck is outside. We go..."
questions.correct_feedback: Right! The taco truck is outside — you go downstairs. / 对！餐车在外面——你们下楼出去。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! The truck is outside the building — you go downstairs and outside. / 餐车在楼外面——你们要下楼出去。

questions.instruction: "Kevin — meet our new neighbor, 4B!" — What does Jake mean?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! Jake is introducing you as the new neighbor. / 对！Jake在介绍你是新邻居。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Meet our new neighbor' means Jake is introducing you to Kevin. / 'Meet our new neighbor' 意思是Jake在把你介绍给Kevin。

options.label: Jake wants to introduce you to Kevin.
Jake wants to have a meeting with Kevin in 4B.
Jake wants to let Kevin fix something for you.

questions.instruction: Kevin offered to help fix things. You want to say thank you and introduce yourself — quick!
questions.correct_feedback: Quick and friendly! You introduced yourself well. / 又快又友好！你很好地介绍了自己。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Kevin is being friendly — 'Thanks! I'm your new neighbor' is the best reply. / Kevin在表示友好——'Thanks! I'm your new neighbor' 是最佳回应。

questions.instruction: Lisa sends "Sunday walk, who's in?" to the building group chat. She lives downstairs with her kid. What is happening?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! In the US, neighbors often plan group activities casually. / 对！在美国，邻居们经常随意组织集体活动。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Who's in?' is a friendly way to invite people to join. It's very common in US neighborhoods. / 'Who's in?'是友好邀请大家参加的方式。在美国小区很常见。

options.label: Lisa invites neighbors to do something together
Lisa is angry at her kid
Lisa wants to move to a new apartment

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! You can introduce yourself to neighbors now. / 完美！你现在能向邻居自我介绍了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! The correct sentence is 'I'm your new neighbor.' / 正确句子是 'I'm your new neighbor.'（我是你的新邻居。）

questions.instruction: 请跟读：Let's go downstairs.
questions.correct_answer: Let's go downstairs.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Great! You can suggest going downstairs naturally. / 发音很棒！你能自然地说'我们下楼吧'了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: Let's GO DOWNstairs. Stress 'go' and 'down'. / 再试一次：重读GO和DOWN。

questions.instruction: "I don't care about the money, Ryan. I just want to SEE you..." — Why does Mrs. Henderson want to see Ryan?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! She's talking to her son, Ryan, and she misses him so much. / 对！她在跟儿子Ryan打电话，她很想他。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Her son' means 她的儿子. She's on the phone with Ryan, her son. / 'Her son' 是她的儿子。她在跟儿子Ryan通话。

options.label: She wants to get money from Ryan.
Her husband wants to see Ryan.

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Nice! Jake's advice: don't ask questions, just be there. / 很好！Jake的建议：别追问，去陪着就好。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Show up' = 去陪陪/出现, 'don't push' = 别追问, 'come in' = 进来。

questions.instruction: 旁白：You knock on her door. Mrs. Henderson opens the door. 你该说什么？
questions.correct_feedback: Sweet! 'I brought you something' is a warm way to offer a gift. / 暖心！'I brought you something' 是送东西时很温暖的说法。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! You have cake for her — 'I brought you something' is the natural thing to say. / 你给她带了蛋糕——'I brought you something' 是最自然的说法。

options.label: I need to close the window.

questions.instruction: "Come in, dear. Sit with me." — What does Mrs. Henderson want you to do?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! She wants your company — come in and sit. / 对！她想要你的陪伴——进来坐坐。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Come in' = 进来, 'sit with me' = 陪我坐坐。She wants you to stay. / 她想让你留下来陪她。

questions.instruction: Mrs. Henderson is sad because her son left. You visit her with cake and watch TV together. You don't ask about the phone call. Is this a good thing to do?
questions.correct_feedback: Exactly! In the US, just 'showing up' for someone is a powerful way to care. / 没错！在美国，'出现在某人身边'本身就是很有力的关心方式。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Sometimes people don't want to talk — just being there shows you care. / 有时候人们不想说话——在旁边陪着就是关心。

options.label: Yes — sometimes being there is enough
No — you should ask many questions

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_feedback: Well done! You can offer a gift naturally now. / 做得好！你现在能自然地表达带了东西给别人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! The correct sentence is 'I brought you something.' 'Brought' is past tense of 'bring'. / 正确句子是 'I brought you something.'（我给你带了东西。）'brought'是'bring'的过去式。

questions.instruction: 请跟读：Come in and sit with me.
questions.correct_answer: Come in and sit with me.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Warm and clear! You sound welcoming. / 温暖又清晰！听起来很热情好客。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: COME in and SIT with me. Stress 'come' and 'sit'. / 再试一次：重读COME和SIT。

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Great! You know the rooms for eating and cooking. / 很好！你知道吃饭和做饭的房间了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Dining room' = 餐厅, 'table' = 桌子, 'kitchen' = 厨房。

questions.instruction: "Kevin likes Lisa. Everyone knows." — What does "everyone knows" mean?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! 'Everyone' means all the people. / 对！'Everyone' 意思是所有人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Everyone knows' = all the people know. It's not a secret! / 'Everyone knows' = 所有人都知道。这不是秘密！

questions.instruction: Mrs. Henderson invited all the neighbors for brunch at her place. People eat, talk, and laugh together. In the US, is this common?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! In the US, neighbors often become like family. Brunch together is very normal! / 对！在美国，邻居常常像家人一样。一起吃早午餐很正常！
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! In the US, neighbors often invite each other for meals. It's a way to build community. / 在美国，邻居们经常互相请吃饭。这是建立社区感的方式。

options.label: Yes — neighbors often share meals together
No — neighbors never talk to each other
No — only family can eat together

questions.instruction: "Kevin pulls you aside in the hall." — What did Kevin do?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! 'Pull aside' means he wants to talk to you privately. / 对！'Pull aside' 意思是他想私下跟你说话。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Pull you aside' = 把你拉到一边，想私下说话。'Push' means the opposite! / 'Pull' 和 'push' 是相反的！

options.label: He closed the door on you

questions.instruction: Kevin: "Was that weird? Am I being weird?" 你该怎么安慰他？
questions.correct_feedback: Thoughtful advice! 'She needs time' is kind and wise. / 很贴心的建议！'She needs time' 既善良又明智。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Kevin is nervous about Lisa — 'She needs time' is the best comfort here. / Kevin在紧张关于Lisa的事——'She needs time' 是最好的安慰。

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_answer: He pulled me aside to talk.
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! 'Pull aside' = take someone to talk privately. / 完美！'Pull aside' = 把某人拉到一边私下说话。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Pulled me aside' is correct — 'pushed' means the opposite direction. / 'Pulled me aside' 才对——'pushed' 是相反方向。

questions.instruction: 请跟读：She needs time.
questions.correct_answer: She needs time.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Clear and gentle! Great advice-giving tone. / 清晰又温和！很好的建议语气。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: She NEEDS TIME. Stress 'needs' and 'time'. / 再试一次：重读NEEDS和TIME。

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Nice! You know the furniture words in Lisa's home. / 很好！你认识Lisa家里的家具词了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Chair = 椅子, table = 桌子, window = 窗户, living room = 客厅。

questions.instruction: "I moved here after my divorce." — What happened to Lisa?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! After divorce, she has no husband anymore. / 对！离婚后，她不再有丈夫了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Divorce' means her marriage ended — she no longer has a husband. / 'Divorce' 意思是婚姻结束了——她不再有丈夫了。

questions.instruction: Lisa says Kevin is great but she's not ready. What do you say?
questions.correct_feedback: Kind and honest! Telling a person how you feel is brave. / 善良又坦诚！告诉一个人你的感受是勇敢的。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Lisa is confused — the best advice is 'tell him how you feel.' / Lisa很迷茫——最好的建议是'告诉他你的感受'。

options.label: Maybe tell him how you feel.
Maybe close the door on him.

questions.instruction: "I started over too." — What does "I started over" mean?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! 'Started over' = began a new life in a new place. / 对！'Started over' = 在新的地方开始新生活。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Started over' means you began again — new place, new life. / 'Started over' 意思是重新开始——新地方、新生活。

options.label: I began a new life in a new place
I went back to my old home

questions.instruction: You see Jake arguing with a man in the hall. What do you do?
questions.correct_feedback: Good instinct! You check on your neighbor. / 好直觉！你去看看邻居的情况。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Jake is your neighbor and friend — you'd walk over to check on him. / Jake是你的邻居和朋友——你会走过去看看他。

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_answer: We sat in chairs by the window.
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! You can describe where you sat at a café. / 完美！你能描述在咖啡馆坐在哪里了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'We sat in chairs by the window.' 'Door' doesn't fit here — they're at the window. / 正确是 'We sat in chairs by the window.'（我们坐在靠窗的椅子上。）

questions.instruction: 请跟读：Maybe tell him how you feel.（重音：tell / feel；语速：慢速，语气温和、像给朋友建议）
questions.correct_answer: Maybe tell him how you feel.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Gentle and clear! You sound like a caring friend. / 温和又清晰！你听起来像个贴心的朋友。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: Maybe TELL him how you FEEL. Stress 'tell' and 'feel'. / 再试一次：重读TELL和FEEL。

questions.instruction: "The company is raising his rent." — What is happening to Jake's apartment?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! 'Raising rent' means the apartment costs more. / 对！'Raising rent' 意思是公寓要花更多钱了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Raising rent' means they want more money for the apartment. / 'Raising rent' 意思是他们要更多的公寓租金。

questions.instruction: Dan shows Jake a contract. Jake looks scared. You need to help — quick!
questions.correct_feedback: Quick thinking! You stepped in to help your neighbor. / 反应快！你站出来帮邻居了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Jake needs help with the letter — 'Let me see the letter' is the right move. / Jake需要信件方面的帮助——'Let me see the letter' 是正确的做法。

questions.instruction: Kevin says "That's how they got Ryan out." Ryan is Mrs. Henderson's son. What does this mean?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! The same thing happened to Ryan before — they raised rent until he left. / 对！同样的事以前发生在Ryan身上——他们涨租直到他离开。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Got Ryan out' means they forced him to leave his apartment by raising rent. / 'Got Ryan out' 意思是他们通过涨租逼Ryan离开了公寓。

options.label: The company used the same way to make Ryan leave
Ryan closed his door and went to sleep
Ryan added new furniture to his flat

questions.instruction: Kevin found a lawyer. Lisa will write. Now it's your turn to help Jake. What do you say?
questions.correct_feedback: Supportive and clear! You're helping Jake explain his problem. / 又支持又清晰！你在帮Jake理清问题。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Jake needs to explain the situation — 'Tell me what happened' is how you help. / Jake需要解释情况——'Tell me what happened' 是你帮忙的方式。

options.label: Open the door for me, slowly.

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Great! 'Person' is one, 'people' is many. / 很好！'Person' 是一个人，'people' 是很多人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Person = 一个人, people = 很多人, neighbor = 邻居。

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_answer: You're the only person I can hear.
questions.correct_feedback: Well done! 'Person' is for one — 'people' is for many. / 做得好！'Person' 指一个人——'people' 指很多人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'The only person' = one person. Use 'person' here, not 'people'. / 'The only person' = 唯一一个人。这里用 'person'，不用 'people'。

questions.instruction: 请跟读：Tell me what happened.（重音：Tell / happened；语速：慢速，语气关心、耐心）
questions.correct_answer: Tell me what happened.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Clear and caring! You sound like a trusted friend. / 清晰又关心！你听起来像一个值得信赖的朋友。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: TELL me what HAPPENED. Stress 'tell' and 'happened'. / 再试一次：重读TELL和HAPPENED。

questions.instruction: "They did the same thing to Ryan. They pushed him out." — What happened to Mrs. Henderson's son?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! 'Pushed out' means they forced her son to leave. / 对！'Pushed out' 意思是他们逼她儿子离开了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Push out' means to force someone to leave — Ryan had to go. / 'Push out' 意思是逼某人离开——Ryan不得不走。

questions.instruction: Mrs. Henderson says her son won't answer. She looks sad. What do you offer?
questions.correct_feedback: Brave and kind! You offered to help a mother call her son. / 又勇敢又善良！你提议帮一位母亲打电话给儿子。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! She needs help calling her son — 'I can call with you' is the best support. / 她需要帮助打电话给儿子——'I can call with you' 是最好的支持。

options.label: I can wash up for you.

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Great! You know the difference between knocking, visiting, and entering. / 很好！你知道敲门、来访和进来的区别了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Knocks on your door' = 敲你的门, 'come round' = 来访, 'come in' = 进来。

questions.instruction: "He's coming to visit next month." — What will Ryan do?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! Ryan will visit his mum — the family will be together again. / 对！Ryan要来看妈妈——一家人又能团聚了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Coming to visit' means Ryan will come to see his mother. / 'Coming to visit' 意思是Ryan会来看望他的母亲。

options.label: He will come to see his mother
He will move to a new flat
He will close his door forever

questions.instruction: You helped Mrs. Henderson call her son. In the US, what does this show about neighbors?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! In the US, neighbors often become your support system — like a second family. / 对！在美国，邻居常常成为你的支持系统——像第二个家。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! In the US, neighbors can be very close — helping each other is what makes a place feel like home. / 在美国，邻居可以非常亲近——互相帮助是让一个地方有家的感觉的原因。

options.label: Good neighbors can become like family
Only family can help with problems

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_answer: They pushed him out of his apartment.
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! 'Push out' = force to leave. 'Pull' is the opposite. / 完美！'Push out' = 逼迫离开。'Pull' 是相反的动作。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Pushed him out' is correct — they forced Ryan to leave. 'Pulled' means the opposite direction. / 'Pushed him out' 才对——他们逼Ryan离开了。'Pulled' 是相反方向。

questions.instruction: 请跟读：I can call with you.（重音：call / you；语速：慢速，语气温暖、给人安心感）
questions.correct_answer: I can call with you.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Warm and steady! You sound supportive. / 温暖又稳定！你听起来很有支持感。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: I can CALL with YOU. Stress 'call' and 'you'. / 再试一次：重读CALL和YOU。

questions.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
questions.correct_feedback: Nice! You can describe an outdoor party scene. / 很好！你能描述户外聚会的场景了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Light = 灯, garden = 花园/院子, gate = 大门, table = 桌子。

questions.instruction: "I want him to meet all of you." — What does Mrs. Henderson want?
questions.correct_feedback: Right! She wants her son to meet all the people here. / 对！她想让儿子认识这里所有的人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Meet all of you' = she wants Ryan to know all the neighbors. / 'Meet all of you' = 她想让Ryan认识所有邻居。

options.label: Ryan to meet all the neighbors

questions.instruction: "Only five of you left under the lights." — Where are the five people?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! They're still outside under the string lights. / 对！他们还在外面的彩灯下。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Under the lights' = outside in the garden where the lights hang. / 'Under the lights' = 在外面彩灯挂着的花园里。

questions.instruction: Jake just told everyone how you helped him. Everyone looks at you. What do you say?
questions.correct_feedback: Humble and warm! That's what neighbors do. / 谦虚又温暖！这就是邻居该做的。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Jake thanked you — 'We're neighbors. That's what we do.' is the heartfelt reply. / Jake在感谢你——'We're neighbors. That's what we do.' 是最真诚的回应。

options.label: We're neighbors. That's what we do.
I want to go upstairs now.
I need to wash the table.

questions.instruction: Lisa says "This place feels like home now." What makes a place feel like home in this story?
questions.correct_feedback: Yes! A home isn't just a place — it's the people in it. / 对！家不只是一个地方——是里面的人。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! In this story, 'home' means feeling safe and cared for by neighbors — not about things. / 在这个故事里，'home' 意味着被邻居关心和安全感——不是关于东西。

questions.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
questions.correct_answer: Lights hang from the garden gate.
questions.correct_feedback: Perfect! You can describe the party scene. / 完美！你能描述聚会场景了。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! 'Lights hang from the garden gate.' The lights are outside, not in the kitchen. / 正确是 'Lights hang from the garden gate.'（彩灯挂在花园大门上。）灯在外面，不在厨房。

questions.instruction: 请跟读：We're neighbors. That's what we do.（重音：neighbors / do；语速：中速，语气温暖、自豪）
questions.correct_answer: We're neighbors. That's what we do.（AI评估发音可懂度）
questions.correct_feedback: Warm and proud! You sound like part of the community. / 温暖又自豪！你听起来像社区的一份子。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Almost! Try again: We're NEIGHBORS. That's what we DO. Stress 'neighbors' and 'do'. / 再试一次：重读NEIGHBORS和DO。
